body.title: Explore: six core prompt patterns in BaseDevice/BaseShim + surface in system behavior
body.result: prompt_builder.py with EXIT_PROTOCOL, FailureClass, tool_choice_for_turn, build_inference_request; BaseDevice helper; palace node written; design doc filed
body.intention: I intend that the six core prompt-engineering patterns (exit protocol, temperature discipline, consequence framing, imperative register, tool-first enforcement, failure-mode naming) become reusable artifacts in BaseDevice so every device inherits correct inference behavior without reimplementing it.
body.description:
DickSimnel's prompt fix revealed six patterns that govern whether OR/Anthropic models follow instructions reliably: (1) exit protocol at top, (2) temperature=0 declared explicitly, (3) consequence framing ('triggers re-dispatch'), (4) imperative register ('output X and stop' not 'you may output X'), (5) tool-first enforcement ('your first action must be a tool call'), (6) failure-mode naming ('any other text is an error'). Explore where these live today (scattered across DS toolloop.py + device.py), whether BaseDevice/BaseShim is the right abstraction layer to carry them, and how surfacing them in system behavior (logs, audit, palace nodes) would close the reinforcement loop.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/device.py (BaseDevice), devices/dicksimnel/device.py, devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py — discovery step will identify others
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; theory-of-operation (every rule carries its why)
**Scope boundary:** IN: design exploration, palace node draft, possibly a base-class prompt-builder mixin. OUT: retrofitting all existing devices in this ticket — that's follow-on work.
**Completion criteria:** Design doc or palace node captures the six patterns with rationale; at minimum a concrete proposal for where in the class hierarchy they live and how a new device would inherit them; CC signs off that the proposal is implementable without breaking existing devices.
